channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: After DeepSeek, the AI giants still have plenty of work left to do
item.description: The tech industry has long been infatuated with metaphors relating to the original space race. Its favorite one is moon shot, a term it applies to any undertaking of atypical ambition.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: AI assistants for lawyers are a booming business—with big risks
item.description: Illinois lawyer Mathew Kerbis markets himself as the Subscription Attorney, charging businesses and individual clients a monthly rate for legal advice and offering additional services like contract review and legal document drafting for a flat fee.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: General purpose AI could lead to array of new risks, experts say in report ahead of AI summit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Advanced artificial intelligence systems have the potential to create extreme new risks, such as fueling widespread job losses, enabling terrorism or running amok, experts said in a first-of-its-kind international report Wednesday cataloging the range of dangers posed by the technology
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tesla's Supercharger network gets faster charging speeds in the US, but not all owners are happy about it
item.description: Tesla says that all North American V4 Superchargers can now deliver up to 325kW… but there’s a catch.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Generative AI: The CIO’s Apollo moment
item.description: The odds of Apollo 11 landing on the moon were shockingly low. Rockets exploded in training, three astronauts were killed in a fire, and Neil Armstrong barely survived a near — fatal test flight.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Zig-Zag (yes, the rolling papers) just launched a turntable right before the Grammys to say: don't you forget about Dre
item.description: Zig-Zag's new turntable isn't made of paper and you shouldn't try to smoke it, but it's here in case you forgot about Dre…
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sophos Report Highlights Cybersecurity Concerns Over GenAI Flaws
item.description: New research from Sophos reveals widespread concern among IT leaders over potential flaws in generative AI systems, despite 99% considering AI essential for cybersecurity provider selection.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Want to try DeepSeek without the privacy worries? Perplexity AI just launched it on its iOS and web apps
item.description: If you subscribe to Perplexity Pro you can now use the R1 model from DeepSeek without any privacy concerns.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: US officials probe China’s DeepSeek AI amid security and privacy scrutiny
item.description: The Chinese AI app DeepSeek has come under intense scrutiny from both the US and European regulators, raising alarms over national security risks, data privacy concerns, and potential intellectual property theft.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Don’t be so quick to click that Google Calendar invite. It could be a hacker’s trap
item.description: A phishing campaign uses Google Calendar to schedule fake meeting invitations that appear legitimate, redirecting targets to phishing sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 4 tiny Docker images for lightweight containers
item.description: Docker images can get pretty bulky if you’re not careful. Your typical Docker image includes everything you need to run a particular piece of software (such as the application language’s runtime) and everything those things depend on.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Doing authentication right
item.description: Any substantial website is going to need to customize itself for individual users. Thus, it is going to have to authenticate those users — that is, let them log in. As a software developer, it is your job to make the whole process as smooth and secure as possible.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tecnología y negocio en el ámbito industrial, la convergencia de dos imprescindibles para Cerealto
item.description: Sobre los hombros de Juan Manuel García Dujo, actual Chief Information Officer (CIO, por sus siglas en inglés) de Cerealto, recae la misión de alinear las TI con los objetivos de negocio.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Max just made a big change, and your streaming life will probably get easier
item.description: Max is making a design change to the homepage of its streaming service by moving the top navigation menu to the left-hand side and tossing in two new options.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Hidden text "salting" is letting hackers craft devious email attacks to evade detection
item.description: An email scanner sees a lot more than the recipient, which can be both a good and a bad thing, experts warn.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: DeepSeek's new AI chatbot and ChatGPT answer sensitive questions about China differently
item.description: Chinese tech startup DeepSeek’s new artificial intelligence chatbot has sparked discussions about the competition between China and the U.S. in AI development, with many users flocking to test the rival of OpenAI's ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says Microsoft is one of the companies eyeing TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday evening that Microsoft is among the U.S. companies looking to take control of TikTok to help the popular app avert an effective ban that could kick-in in April.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DeepSeek's rise spooks investors, threatens to upend AI
item.description: The rise in popularity of a high-performing and cheaply built Chinese artificial intelligence (AI) model has shaken the confidence of investors, while raising larger questions about the future of American-made AI and upping the stakes of Washington’s tech rivalry with Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How to pick the right SAST tool
item.description: Like pretty much any security tool, picking a SAST (static application security testing) tool isn’t a matter of “What’s the best?” but rather “What’s the best for me?” Whether you already have a tool that needs replacing or you’re starting from greenfield, it’s helpful to understand the market so yo
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Sweden seizes vessel suspected of 'sabotage' after undersea data cable rupture in Baltic Sea
item.description: RIGA, Latvia (AP) — Swedish prosecutors announced Sunday night that they have opened a preliminary investigation into suspected aggravated “sabotage” and ordered the detention of a vessel in the Baltic Sea suspected of damaging an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: New federal CIO onboard at OMB
item.description: Greg Barbaccia takes over for Clare Martorana as the government’s lead technology executive. The post New federal CIO onboard at OMB first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: People are hawking TikTok-loaded phones for thousands on eBay, Facebook
item.description: People are listing phones preloaded with TikTok for tens of thousands of dollars on eBay, Facebook marketplace and other online storefronts — though it is not clear if there are many buyers at those prices. TikTok was briefly unavailable to U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Robotic dog helps those facing mental health and cognitive challenges
item.description: A U.S. robotics company has developed "Jennie," a robotic pet powered by artificial intelligence that's designed to comfort those facing mental health challenges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The sickening truth: Healthcare data breaches reach all-time high
item.description: Healthcare records for more than 184 million Americans were breached in 2024, but you can take steps to minimize that risk. Kurt the CyberGuy explains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan's parliament passes bill with sweeping controls on social media
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistan’s lower house of parliament on Thursday passed a controversial bill that will give the government sweeping controls on social media, including sending users to prison for spreading disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, NewsNation
